title: [Full Stack] Next.js 15 + Supabase Real-Time Usage Dashboard
description: Build a real-time usage analytics dashboard using Next.js 15 App Router and Supabase — covering SSR data fetching, real-time subscriptions, and row-level security.

steps.title: Set up Next.js 15 + Supabase project
steps.instruction: Create a new Next.js 15 project with Supabase integration.
steps.prompt: Create a new Next.js 15 project with TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, and Supabase integration. Include: Supabase client setup for both server and client components, environment variable configuration, and a basic layout with auth context.
steps.expected_result: A running Next.js 15 app with Supabase client configured for both SSR and client-side use
steps.tip: Use `npx create-next-app@latest` and select TypeScript + Tailwind. Then install `@supabase/ssr` for Next.js 15 compatibility.

steps.title: Create the Supabase schema
steps.instruction: Design and create the database schema for the usage analytics dashboard.
steps.prompt: Create a Supabase migration for a usage analytics table that tracks: user_id, event_type (page_view, feature_used, api_call), metadata (JSONB), timestamp, and session_id. Include indexes for fast queries and RLS policies.
steps.expected_result: A Supabase migration file that creates the analytics table with proper indexes and RLS
steps.tip: JSONB for metadata allows flexible event tracking without schema changes for new event types

steps.title: Build SSR dashboard page
steps.instruction: Create the main dashboard page using Next.js 15 Server Components to fetch and display usage data.
steps.prompt: Build a Next.js 15 Server Component dashboard page that: fetches today's usage stats from Supabase (total events, unique users, most used features), displays them in stat cards, and shows a table of the last 10 events. Use Tailwind for styling.
steps.expected_result: A server-rendered dashboard page showing live usage statistics
steps.tip: Use `createServerClient` from `@supabase/ssr` in Server Components — never use the browser client on the server

steps.title: Add real-time updates
steps.instruction: Add a client component that subscribes to Supabase real-time and updates the dashboard live.
steps.prompt: Create a Next.js 15 Client Component that subscribes to the analytics table using Supabase real-time. When a new event arrives, update a live counter and add it to a recent events list without page refresh.
steps.expected_result: The dashboard updates in real-time as new events are recorded, without page reloads
steps.tip: Wrap the real-time component in a Suspense boundary so SSR content loads first

challenge: Add authentication so users can only see their own usage data. Implement a shareable public dashboard URL using a signed URL pattern that grants read-only access to specific data.
